TextENGNew: Walking side by side, Chisato and I headed toward the station where Tamako was waiting.
I matched my pace to Chisato's brisk stride, but my mind was utterly consumed with what Toudou-kun had said.
"Ever since you transferred here...@k@vS04/39/310200049. I haven't been able to stop thinking about you..."
"Until now, I didn't have the courage, but...@k@vS04/39/310200051. I'm... in love with you, Kimiyoshi."
Toudou-kun was in love... with me...!
changeReason: Changes 'likes me' to 'was in love with me', significantly intensifying the emotion; Japanese 好き in this romantic confession context supports the stronger reading.

TextENGNew: No matter how many times I replayed it, I still couldn't believe it! Toudou-kun... Toudou-kun felt the same way I did — he actually had feelings for me...!
......It was like a dream!
A truth that felt like fiction... Please, let this be real — not a dream, not a lie... My steps were so light I felt like I was floating, and my heart was so giddy it seemed ready to take flight.
"Sommmebody's looking awfully happy, hmm?"
Chisato poked me in the cheek.
changeReason: Corrects physical action from 'pinched my cheeks' to 'poked me in the cheek'; Japanese つつく means to poke/prod, not pinch.

TextENGNew: "Th-that's... that's not true...!!"

Had she seen through me — realized I'd been thinking about Toudou-kun? ...And yet, oddly enough, being caught in my flustered state felt almost pleasant. Was this what it meant to have feelings you couldn't contain?
"Everything I say goes in one ear and out the other.@k@vS04/37/310300361. Natsumi, 'peak happiness' is written all over your face, you know?@k@vS04/37/310300362. Heh heh heh!"
``But you know, ever since you transferred here, there's always been this little hint of unease about you.@k@vS04/37/310300364. Seeing you looking this happy makes me feel relieved too!``
changeReason: New version preserves the Japanese temporal emphasis that Natsumi has always had a persistent hint of unease since transferring.

TextENGNew: "You think so...?@k@vS04/36/310100459. Really...?"
"Of course~!@k@vS04/37/310300366. How many times do I have to tell you?@k@vS04/37/310300367. I consider myself your very best friend!"
"...Thank you, Chisato."
I really was happy...!!
The boy I liked had confessed to me, and my dearest friend was cheering my love on...
...Ahhh~ It was great to be alive!!
``But don't you dare treasure only Akira and forget about us — I won't forgive you!@k@vS04/37/310300369. If you get so swept up that you leave me behind, I'll haunt you as a lonely ghost~!``
changeReason: Corrects 'I'll end up a lonely ghost' to 'I'll haunt you as a lonely ghost'; Japanese 化けて出てやる is an active threat, not a passive outcome.

TextENGNew: Chisato caught me in a headlock. W-wait~! If you squeeze that hard, I'll run out of air and pass out~!
"Ahahaha!@k@vS04/37/310300371. Don't go fainting with that blissful look on your face, Miss Requited Love!"
"Pwaah?@k@vS04/36/310100464. C-come on already——"
Chisato loosened her grip, and I wriggled free. ...When I looked around, I realized we'd already arrived at the station without my noticing.
It was as though I'd floated here on a carpet of happiness — the walk had passed in the blink of an eye.
changeReason: New version preserves the 'carpet of happiness' metaphor and adds the temporal detail of the walk passing 'in the blink of an eye,' present in Japanese but lost in old.

TextENGNew: "...Oh, over there."
Tamako was waiting for us by the bronze statue at the station — our usual meeting spot. Spotting her, I raised my hand and called out.
changeReason: New adds 'our usual meeting spot' detail present in Japanese 待ち合わせ目印 but omitted in old.

TextENGNew: "Heeey, Tamako-ch——"
``Yo, sorry for the wait!``
changeReason: Changes 'thanks for waiting' to 'sorry for the wait'; Japanese お待たせ is an apology, not thanks.

TextENGNew: "Eek!@k@vS04/38/310400134. Oh, it's just you, Chisato.@k@vS04/38/310400135. You startled me~!!"
Chisato slipped past me, ran up to Tamako, and gave her a pat on the shoulder. Tamako pressed a hand to her chest as if genuinely startled.
changeReason: Old omits that Chisato 'slipped past me' (私の脇をするりと抜けて), a narrative detail present in Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Tamako, sorry we're late!``
changeReason: Changes singular 'I'm sorry for being late' to plural 'sorry we're late', changing who is apologizing.

TextENGNew: Still wearing the afterglow of Toudou-kun's confession on my face, I pressed my palms together in apology.
"No, it's fine.@k@vS04/38/310400137. I just got here myself."
"Well then, let's go!@k@vS04/38/310400139. Let's find somewhere that'll really lift Natsumi's spirits!@k@vS04/38/310400140. How about some sweet ice cream to blow all your worries away?"
changeReason: Corrects mistranslation: old says Natsumi is already cheerful; Japanese says the intent is to find somewhere to lift her spirits.

TextENGNew: Tamako put extra emphasis on her words, as if convinced that sheer energy could cheer me up. Drawn in by her smile, I smiled right back.
``Oh!@k@vS04/37/310300374. Come to think of it, today Natsumi promised to treat us, right?``
changeReason: Changes 'treat me' (singular) to 'treat us' (plural), correcting who receives the treat.

TextENGNew: "Well then — no holding back! Let's eat big!!"
``I'm in!@k@vS04/36/310100469. Ice cream, let's go, let's go~!!``
changeReason: Changes 'Indeed!' to 'I'm in!'; Japanese さんせーい (agreement/approval) better matched by 'I'm in.'

TextENGNew: Chisato and I were bouncing around with huge grins on our faces. Watching us, Tamako looked a little puzzled.
``...Hey, Natsumi?@k@vS04/38/310400143. Did something happen just now?``
changeReason: Changes 'earlier' to 'just now'; Japanese さっき means a moment ago, a more recent timeframe.

TextENGNew: "Huh?@k@vS04/36/310100471. What makes you say that?"
"Well, you were completely down in the dumps a little while ago, and now you look like you're at the absolute peak of happiness.@k@vS04/38/310400145. So I figured something must have happened."
Tamako stared at my face with a quizzical look, seemingly bewildered by my unexpected buoyancy.
"Hu~uh?!@k@vS04/36/310100473. Do I?@k@vS04/36/310100474. I think you're imagining things."
Even as the words left my mouth, a laugh bubbled up.
changeReason: Old says laughter burst out after speaking; new correctly captures laughter emerging while still speaking, matching Japanese 言っているそばから.

TextENGNew: Oh... that's right. Chisato had just told me — no more secrets. I needed to tell Tamako about what happened with Toudou-kun.
But it was a little embarrassing... It would sound like bragging...
Oh, but Tamako already knew I liked Toudou-kun, didn't she? ...In that case, I'd just say it. No holding back.
changeReason: Changes 'With pride' to 'No holding back'; Japanese 思い切って means decisively/taking the plunge.

TextENGNew: Would Tamako... be as happy for me as Chisato had been? Would she encourage me...?
——But the very next moment.
``You see, Natsumi...@k@vS04/37/310300377. she's in love with Akira.``
changeReason: Changes 'Natsumi likes Akira' to 'she's in love with Akira', intensifying from casual to romantic.

TextENGNew: Like being doused with ice water without warning, my entire body froze. ...That was Chisato's voice.
Cold, mechanical... that same cruel tone from before echoed in my ears.
Huh...? Chisato...? Why did that sound so cold? ...What?
You said you'd support my love... didn't you? Just now? Because we're best friends...
Huh...? What...?
Chisato, what you said before — was it all a lie...?
...That you'd known I liked Akira all along. That you and Tamako had been waiting for me to tell you...
changeReason: Changes 'suspected' to 'known all along'; Japanese お見通し means completely seen through.

TextENGNew: When I looked up, Tamako was staring at me with a smile frozen stiffly on her face, her eyes slightly unfocused.
changeReason: New adds 'her eyes slightly unfocused' (焦点のぼやけた目), a detail present in Japanese but omitted in old.

TextENGNew: ``Ahaha, is that so?@k@vS04/38/310400147. Natsumi has a thing for Akira.@k@vS04/38/310400148. ...Hmm.``
changeReason: Changes tone from confirmatory 'so that's it' to questioning 'is that so?'; shifts Tamako's reaction.

TextENGNew: "...I thought as much.@k@vS04/38/310400150. So...?@k@vS04/38/310400151. ...Does that have anything to do with why you were late to meet us?"
Tamako asked in a voice wrapped in warmth. ...But her eyes held not the faintest hint of a smile — only a cold light that seemed to wish me not a shred of happiness.
"It's nothing!@k@vS04/36/310100476. It has nothing to do with that!"
I clowned around, trying to play it off. ...But why? Was being late really that unforgivable?
If that was the problem, I'd apologize. Please, Tamako. Don't be angry...!
"Then... why were you late?"

changeReason: Changes interjection from 'Uh...' to 'Oh...'; Japanese あ is surprise/realization.

TextENGNew: "It has nothing to do with Akira, right?@k@vS04/38/310400154. So why were you late?@k@vS04/38/310400155. Tell me.@k@vS04/38/310400156. You do have a reason, don't you?"
"Th-that's...@k@vS04/36/310100479. It's, um...@k@vS04/36/310100480. I sort of forgot my homework notebook... so I went back to get it——"
The lie tumbled out before I could stop it. But I instantly realized it would be seen through, and regretted it.
...What was I saying? Chisato knew that Toudou-kun and I had been alone together in the classroom.
Which meant she must have been watching us from outside the classroom...?
That was it. ...Chisato had to know everything. She might have even seen Toudou-kun stumbling out in embarrassment after his confession.
And after that — me, alone in the classroom with a radiant smile on my face...
Connecting those two dots made it easy to guess what had happened. And in fact, Chisato had guessed exactly right.
...And then it hit me — the reason Chisato's face had been so tense when we met in the hallway.
Could it be... no, it couldn't.
The reason her very first words had been an interrogation about my crush... was it because Chisato was also in love with Toudou-kun...?
changeReason: Old says 'asked'; new says 'interrogation'; Japanese 詰問 means interrogation/cross-examination.

TextENGNew: Then Chisato wasn't actually happy for me at all... and that was why Tamako was being cold, and more than that — did they actually... resent me...?
"Hey, Chisato..."
"Natsumi, you're such a scatterbrain.@k@vS04/37/310300380. You really can't be forgetting things like that."
Was Chisato covering for me? She laughed casually, as if nothing was wrong. But that laughter was unnaturally bright... so I—
changeReason: Old says 'put two and two together'; new says 'covering for me'; Japanese 話をあわせてくれたのか means went along with the story.

TextENGNew: "Y... yeah!"
I matched her energy as best I could and replied with forced cheer. ...But inside, fear and doubt were churning in a dizzying spiral.
changeReason: Changes 'anxiety' to 'doubt', different emotional register fitting the context.

TextENGNew: ``Well, at least you realized you'd forgotten your notebook before getting too far.``
changeReason: Old says 'before you got home'; new says 'before getting too far'; Japanese 途中で means along the way.

TextENGNew: "Y-yeah, really..."
Tamako placed a caring hand on my shoulder and gave me a gentle smile. ...But the moment her hand touched me, an inexplicable chill raced through my entire body, and I shuddered.
changeReason: New adds the trigger 'the moment her hand touched me' for the chill, present in Japanese.

TextENGNew: I didn't know. What kind of face was I supposed to make for them...?
Just moments ago... I'd been at the peak of happiness.
These two were my precious friends. Since we'd met, they'd taught me so much and helped me in so many ways.
changeReason: Old adds 'worked together' not in Japanese; new more accurately translates as 'helped me in so many ways.'

TextENGNew: ...So why did being with them now feel so unbearable?
"Hey, Chisato...@k@vS04/36/310100485. About Toudou-kun... by any chance, do you..."
"Yeah, yeah.@k@vS04/37/310300382. ...Natsumi?@k@vS04/37/310300383. I get that you're on cloud nine, but honestly, I don't think going on and on about it is such a great idea."
changeReason: Corrects mistranslation: old misses that Chisato is telling Natsumi to stop going on about the topic.

TextENGNew: "Huh——"
My words weren't so much unheard as outright rejected.
Chisato's eyes were terrifyingly cold.
changeReason: Old says 'surprisingly scary'; new says 'terrifyingly cold', better capturing the chilling quality.

TextENGNew: She seemed like a completely different person from just minutes ago. I didn't know what to do.
"Chisato, I didn't mean it like that..."
"Okay, that's enough!@k@vS04/37/310300385. No more of that topic for today!"
"Got it?"
She cut me off sharply and strode away. ...It felt as if all the happiness I'd been carrying had been swatted aside without a second thought.
changeReason: Changes 'shrill words' to 'cut me off sharply'; Japanese ピシャリ means curtly, not shrilly.

TextENGNew: Why...? Why had things suddenly become like this...? You said you'd support me... said you were happy for me... so why?!
"...Let's get going.@k@vS04/38/310400159. Since it's the beginning of the month, maybe they have new flavors."
Tamako spoke up, trying to lift the mood. Hearing that same gentle voice, I felt just a small measure of relief.
"Tamako..."
"Natsumi.@k@vS04/38/310400161. No matter what happens, we're on your side!@k@vS04/38/310400162. I'm rooting for your love!@k@vS04/38/310400163. Go get 'em!"
"Yeah.@k@vS04/36/310100490. Thanks! Tamako-ch...!"
——And yet.
Without waiting for my reply, Tamako quickly trotted ahead and fell into step beside Chisato. ...My bright, earnest response was left abandoned on the street behind me.
I followed a step behind the two of them as they walked side by side, an uncomfortable knot in my chest.
A thought suddenly came to me.
What if Chisato — or maybe even Tamako — was also in love with Toudou-kun...? Was that what this was — jealousy...?
changeReason: Changes 'liked Toudou-kun' to 'in love with Toudou-kun', significant intensity change.

TextENGNew: The moment that thought took shape... a fresh wave of anxiety crashed over me.
If these two turned against me, I'd be shut out.
changeReason: Changes 'end our friendship' to 'shut out'; Japanese 仲間はずれ specifically means exclusion/ostracism.

TextENGNew: ...If that happened, I'd be isolated at school. Going to the bathroom, eating lunch, moving between classrooms — all of it, alone. ...No. That was absolutely unacceptable!
...But I loved Toudou-kun...
changeReason: Changes 'I like Toudou-kun' to 'I loved Toudou-kun'; both tense and intensity change.

TextENGNew: What should I do?
What on earth... was I supposed to do now...?
"Hm?@k@vS04/37/310300388. Natsumi, you're looking down again."
Chisato glanced back with a worried expression. Tamako stopped too, tilting her head and peering at me with concern.
"You okay? Feeling unwell?"
Both of them searched my eyes with what looked like genuine worry. ...Could I truly believe in their kindness...?
"No, I'm fine, I'm fine.@k@vS04/36/310100493. Maybe my hormones are out of whack lately~.@k@vS04/36/310100494. I seem to bounce between happy and depressed on a dime.@k@vS04/36/310100495. Ehehe."
"What?!@k@|@y@vS04/37/310300390. ...Wait — Natsumi, could you be going through menopause?!"
"Ahahahaha."
It wasn't a particularly funny joke, but I laughed as hard as I could anyway.
We were close friends. Always together. A fun little group. ...Right?
"There you go — you're finally back to your usual self.@k@vS04/38/310400166. You were just so stressed about the career interview that you went a little haywire!"
"Ehehe. Maybe so.@k@vS04/36/310100498. No point in worrying about the future, right?"
"We're done thinking about careers for today.@k@vS04/37/310300392. Come on — ice cream, ice cream!"
Chisato practically danced her way into the ice cream shop.
Compared to the turmoil of my budding romance with Toudou-kun, career worries felt like a trivial matter... but I'd let it stand as the excuse.
changeReason: New adds the critical detail of using career stress as a cover story, which old omits.

TextENGNew: What mattered most was staying close with Chisato and Tamako — the three of us, always together!
The moment we stepped into the ice cream shop, our spirits lifted instantly.
In truth, as I gazed at the showcase lined with row upon row of colorful ice cream... even the tension with my friends started to feel like it didn't matter.
changeReason: Old says 'friends' consideration'; new correctly says 'tension with friends'; Japanese 気を遣う means being cautious/tense.

TextENGNew: I'd give Toudou-kun my answer tomorrow, or the day after — soon. And then we'd go see lots of movies together.
I'd blurted out that I liked happy endings, but honestly, I'd be happy watching anything as long as it was with him. In fact, a scary movie might be even better.
Holding hands during the scary parts while we watched... Just kidding~
Lost in those thoughts, I was getting carried away with my own fantasies. And this time, so they wouldn't catch on—
``I'll have the mint chocolate and banana-and-strawberry double in a waffle cone, please!``
changeReason: New correctly identifies the order as a double scoop (Ｗ), a detail old misses.

TextENGNew: Chisato and Tamako, already holding their cones, looked over at me shouting my order and shared a quiet laugh.
changeReason: Old says 'laughed hard'; new says 'shared a quiet laugh'; Japanese クスリ means a soft chuckle.

TextENGNew: Licking our ice cream, the three of us strolled along side by side.
The sweet, cold ice cream swept away all the awkwardness from before, carrying it off to some distant shore of happiness. ...Girls could survive on sweets alone. Surely that was a universal truth.
Hahhh~~, so goood~~! My heart was filling up with bliss all over again.
"The Honey Crunch here really is the best, isn't it?"
``Honey Crunch and... is the other one the new flavor?``
changeReason: Old says both flavors are new; new correctly asks if only the other one is new.

TextENGNew: "That's right.@k@vS04/37/310300395. Watermelon Ribbon.@k@vS04/37/310300396. It's tasty too, but Honey Crunch is simply non-negotiable!"
"You think?@k@vS04/38/310400169. Swiss Chocolate Fudge is my favorite.@k@vS04/38/310400170. What about you, Natsumi?"
"Mint chocolate is my favorite!"
I answered brightly, matching the bounce of their conversation. But then—
"Huhhh~?@k@vS04/37/310300398. Mint chocolate? Such an old-fashioned flavor — I'm surprised you never get tired of it~!"
"Huh... old-fashioned?"

Caught off guard by the response, I looked down at my ice cream. Chocolate chips danced through the vivid pale blue — it looked undeniably delicious, the very color of happiness. ...What part of this was old-fashioned?
changeReason: Changes 'lumps' to 'chips' and 'teal' to 'pale blue' for 水色, different visual descriptions.

TextENGNew: Of course it wasn't, rationally speaking — but feeling like she was calling me old-fashioned cast a shadow of unease over my heart.
Was this a repeat of what happened earlier? ...No, it couldn't be.
But Chisato, as if oblivious to my feelings, calmly continued.

"Mint chocolate is, well... more than old-fashioned, it's kind of... rural.@k@vS04/37/310300400. Don't you think it's a bit, I don't know, unexciting?@k@vS04/37/310300401. It's been everywhere since forever."
``Flavors like Honey Crunch and Berry Berry Cheesecake — those are the kind you only find in cities like Tokyo, worldwide!``
changeReason: New adds 'worldwide' (全世界でも), present in Japanese but omitted in old.

TextENGNew: ``I guess...``
changeReason: Changes from questioning 'You think so?' to tentative agreement 'I guess...'; different response type.

TextENGNew: "Exactly!@k@vS04/37/310300404. Seeking out new flavors, pioneering, always pushing forward!@k@vS04/37/310300405. That's the right way to stay on the cutting edge of trends!"
Chisato said it with a touch of pride. To me, her words sounded sharper than usual — like pointed criticism aimed directly at me.
...I didn't like this side of Chisato. She probably didn't mean it that way, but it came across as boasting about city life, with a barb directed squarely at me.
Having been born and raised in Okinomiya — not exactly the middle of nowhere, but hardly a city either — I couldn't help but feel a sting of frustration every time Chisato made comments like these.
Of course, that might just have been my own insecurities talking, but still...
Perhaps sensing how I felt, Tamako put on a conciliatory smile and changed the subject.
"Hey Natsumi, what kind of place did you live in before you came here?"
"Huh?"
"You know, when you introduced yourself, you mentioned the name of your old town, right?@k@vS04/38/310400173. I didn't really catch it at the time, but I've been wanting to ask."
``Ohh, that's right, that's right.@k@vS04/37/310300407. I want to hear about it too.@k@|@y@vS04/37/310300408. So, what was it like?``
changeReason: New adds 'I've been wanting to ask' present in Japanese but omitted in old.

TextENGNew: "Um, well..."
I took a bite of my mint chocolate, pretending to consider my answer. Meanwhile, I was privately deciding to stop eating mint chocolate.
At least — absolutely never in front of Toudou-kun.
——Just then, a voice from somewhere reached my ears, and... I froze.
'...As stated, we ask all residents of Shishibone City and surrounding areas to please remain calm...'
It was a newscaster's voice, drifting from a television displayed in an electronics shop window. ...And on the screen was a scene I recognized.
"Natsumi...?"
I stopped in my tracks and stared at the screen, transfixed. Then I gasped.
changeReason: Old says 'gazed like devouring'; new says 'transfixed'; Japanese 食い入るように means staring intently.

TextENGNew: What the television was showing... was Grandma's countryside. Hinamizawa village.
When I was little, visiting Grandma's hometown, we'd gone up to a shrine with a beautiful hilltop view and taken a family photo. That picture still sat on the dresser in Grandma's room.
The view on the television was the same — the mountain vista from Furude Shrine. ...Except that low clouds hung over everything, shrouding the scene in a white, fog-like haze.
changeReason: Old implies multiple scenes; new correctly says this specific view matched her memory.

TextENGNew: ......Fog? No — this wasn't fog...?!

``I repeat:@k@vS04/00/VT1B_Tvann1003. earlier today, a large-scale gas disaster occurred in the Hinamizawa district of Shishibone City.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_Tvann1004. Numerous casualties and missing persons have been reported.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_Tvann1005. At present, no survivors have been found.``
changeReason: Changes 'near Shishibone City' to 'of Shishibone City', correcting the geographic relationship.

TextENGNew: ...No survivors have been found.
Those merciless words sent dread crawling up my spine.
Did that mean... the entire village had been wiped out in a single night...?!
changeReason: Old states destruction as fact; new preserves the questioning/disbelieving tone from Japanese.

TextENGNew: And it was the village right next to Okinomiya — the town where I was born and raised...?!
A gas disaster...? What did that even mean...? Why...?
Hinamizawa meant... my relative who served as the village head — was he dead too...?
changeReason: Old adds 'distant' relative not in Japanese, and uses 'mayor' vs more accurate 'village head' for 村長.

TextENGNew: Almost my entire extended family lived in Hinamizawa village. Those relatives... the Kimiyoshi clan... all of them... gone overnight.
All the strength drained from my body. I barely kept myself from collapsing. The stumble sent my mint chocolate tumbling to the ground with a wet splat.
...Wait — what about Okinomiya?
And the daughters of the Three Families... Mion, Rika — what about them...?!
"Natsumi, what's wrong?@k@vS04/38/310400175. Are you okay?@k@vS04/38/310400176. Is this news somehow connected to you?"
"No, it's nothing.@k@vS04/36/310100511. Sorry — I just remembered something I need to do.@k@vS04/36/310100512. I have to head home."
I forced an awkward smile without meeting their eyes. ...Right now, I couldn't bear to talk with them.
changeReason: Old says 'wasn't in the mood'; new says 'couldn't bear to talk'; Japanese とてもなれなかった suggests stronger inability.

TextENGNew: "Wait, Natsumi...?!"
"Yeah, sorry.@k@vS04/36/310100514. I'm fine."
The devastating news gave me an escape — I broke away from the strained atmosphere with Chisato and Tamako and fled straight home.
changeReason: New correctly captures that the tragic news provided an escape/excuse to leave.
